xG From Scratch & the Finishing-Skill Question

Calibrated probabilistic prediction on StatsBomb Open Data

Author

Alejandro Izaguirre

Published

June 15, 2026

Data & exclusions

StatsBomb Open Data (event + freeze-frame), pulled as raw JSON. Shots are the unit of analysis. Penalties are excluded (a different process); direct free kicks are kept but flagged. shot_statsbomb_xg is retained purely as a benchmark and is never a feature.

shots goals base_rate players matches competitions
19887 2085 0.105 2521 800 6

Class balance by competition:

competition shots goals base_rate
La Liga 9356 1058 0.1130825
FA Women’s Super League 5869 636 0.1083660
FIFA World Cup 2012 156 0.0775348
UEFA Euro 1269 111 0.0874704
Women’s World Cup 1195 105 0.0878661
Champions League 186 19 0.1021505

EDA

The visual hook — goal rate over pitch location — plus the empirical goal-rate curves against the two dominant geometric features.

Models & leakage discipline

Three models on identical match-grouped CV folds (shots from one match never span train/test), with out-of-fold predictions for every shot:

  • A — logistic: distance + angle + body part (interpretable baseline).
  • B — GAM: mgcv 2-D spatial smooth s(x, y) + context terms.
  • C — xgboost: full feature set, nrounds chosen by inner CV.
model log_loss brier
statsbomb_xg 0.2734619 0.0776856
xgboost 0.2758268 0.0788739
gam 0.2832102 0.0800803
logistic 0.2940126 0.0840472

Calibration & benchmark vs StatsBomb

Great ranking is not enough — predicted probabilities must be calibrated. Reliability diagram (out-of-fold), with ECE and the Murphy/Brier decomposition.

model log_loss brier ece reliability resolution uncertainty
logistic 0.2940126 0.0840472 0.0066666 0.0001820 0.0093023 0.0938504
gam 0.2832102 0.0800803 0.0029424 0.0001599 0.0132937 0.0938504
xgboost 0.2758268 0.0788739 0.0032474 0.0000522 0.0144093 0.0938504
statsbomb_xg 0.2734619 0.0776856 0.0064596 0.0001155 0.0155380 0.0938504

If the GBM is miscalibrated, leakage-safe isotonic recalibration (fit on other folds, applied out-of-fold):

metric xgb_before xgb_after
ECE 0.0032474 0.0039271
log_loss 0.2758268 0.2803052
brier 0.0788739 0.0792673

Framing: the question is “how close can open features get to a production model,” not “I beat StatsBomb.” The gap to statsbomb_xg is reported honestly above.

Finishing skill: real or regression to the mean?

Per-player goals-above-xG, then a hierarchical shrinkage model (glmer, player random intercept, model-xG as a logit-scale offset). Low-volume players are pulled toward zero.

Top finishers by shrunken skill (with 95% intervals):

player_name shots goals xg gax skill_logit ci
Alexis Alejandro Sánchez Sánchez 52 17 7.4 9.6 0.260 [-0.09, 0.61]
Lauren James 81 16 7.5 8.5 0.219 [-0.12, 0.56]
Thierry Henry 72 19 10.4 8.6 0.215 [-0.13, 0.56]
Lionel Andrés Messi Cuccittini 1001 168 120.6 47.4 0.181 [0.02, 0.35]
Samuel Eto’’o Fils 130 35 23.9 11.1 0.181 [-0.12, 0.48]
Vivianne Miedema 156 35 23.8 11.2 0.155 [-0.14, 0.44]
Francesc Fàbregas i Soler 62 14 8.7 5.3 0.119 [-0.23, 0.46]
Lauren Hemp 81 16 10.1 5.9 0.119 [-0.22, 0.45]
Khadija Monifa Shaw 70 13 7.7 5.3 0.118 [-0.23, 0.46]
Katie McCabe 73 10 5.4 4.6 0.116 [-0.24, 0.47]

Split-half persistence test (odd/even shots; permutation null):

n_players split_half_r perm_p
79 0.2132479 0.055

Verdict: across 79 players, split-half correlation of finishing over-performance is r = 0.213 (permutation p = 0.055). We cannot reject that finishing over-performance in this sample is mostly noise.

Limitations

  • Selection bias: open data skews toward elite competitions; finishing estimates may not transfer to lower levels.
  • No tracking data: freeze frames are a snapshot at shot moment, not full positioning; defender/GK features are approximations.
  • Sample size: the finishing-skill verdict is bounded by shots-per-player; the shrinkage and persistence results are about this sample.
  • statsbomb_xg is a black box we benchmark against, not ground truth.
Note

StatsBomb data is free for non-commercial use. Attribution: data provided by StatsBomb (https://github.com/statsbomb/open-data).
